title: Dynamic import() into your Ember app
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: A screencast where we build an app that loads real data, lazily loads Highcharts on demand, and plots the data using Highcharts.

title: Ember 2018 Priorities

summary: We need to ship what we started and let our newer contributors shine and lead.

title: Living Animation
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: What ambitious web application developers need to learn from game developers. Presented at EmberConf 2018 in Portland.

title: Design for Learning
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: This is a wide-ranging talk on learning and why you need to understand how people learn if you want to build good software. Plus I build a fun orbital mechanics demo. Presented at EmberNYC, March 2018.

title: How to Add TailwindCSS to an Ember App
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: There is definitely an intimidation factor some people feel about integrating things into Ember apps. title: Mind and Hand
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: "Mens et Manus" (latin for "Mind and Hand") is the official motto of MIT. Lots of schools have Latin mottoes, and they are usually pretty bland. But mens et manus was — and still is — a radical social statement. It says that knowing and doing

title: Effective team culture: Five Whys & Stop-the-line
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: When Google set out to measure what makes their best software development teams successful, they found a strong correlation with psychological safety: A shared belief held by members of a team that the team is safe for interpersonal risk-taking... title: Search-first Web Applications
author: Edward Faulkner

title: CSS and Markup in Javascript is an Evolutionary Dead End
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: There is a popular trend among some Javascript developers to argue that humans should stop writing CSS and HTML (and HTML-based templates). title: Cardstack Architecture Notes
author: Edward Faulkner

title: Lake Wobegon Web Development
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: There is a strain of thought in the web development community that believes there's a tension between having comprehensive developer tools and delivering good user experiences: This is exactly backwards. At the scale of the entire web, better tools are the only way to deliver better experiences. The

title: Strong Conventions Make Hiring Easier
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: The most reliable way to evaluate programmer candidates is to hire them to do a bit of realistic work. This is widely understood, but not widely practiced. The biggest barrier is finding projects for them to work on. In most organizations, the overhead of getting a new person started is

title: Frameworks are where we iterate on tomorrow's universal abstractions
author: Edward Faulkner
summary: The amount of complexity a single human programmer can handle is fundamentally limited. Until our brave cyborg future comes, even the best programmers can only hold so much in their minds at once.
